title: lathyrus palustris subsp. pilosus
intro: Lathyrus palustris subsp. pilosus, also known as Marsh Pea or Marsh Lathyrus, is a perennial legume plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that thrives in wet environments. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Lathyrus palustris subsp. pilosus include its ability to support kidney function, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its toxicity and potential side effects should be carefully considered before use.
